description: A consumer spawns one task per message with no limit, exhausting the DB connection pool or memory.
body: Failure mode: work is pulled and processed with unbounded concurrency, so a burst opens more DB connections or holds more buffers than the pool allows, starving the service and its neighbors. Detection signals: a goroutine/promise per message with no semaphore; connection-pool-timeout errors under load; memory grows with queue depth. Do not fan out without a bound. Safer fallback: cap concurrency to downstream capacity, apply backpressure by pausing fetch at the limit, and load-test that in-flight work plateaus.
